The Union recognizes special position of Buddhism as the faith professed by the great majority of the citizens of the Union.
The Union also recognizes Christianity, Islam, Hinduism and Animism as the religions existing in the Union at the day of the coming into operation of this Constitution.
The Union may assist and protect the religions it recognizes to its utmost.

Every citizen has the duty to uphold: non-disintegration of the Union; non-disintegration of national solidarity; perpetuation of sovereignty. a. non-disintegration of the Union; b. non-disintegration
Every citizen has duty to abide by the provisions of this Constitution.
Every citizen has the duty to safeguard independence, sovereignty and territorial integrity of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar.
Every citizen has the duty to undergo military training in accord with the provisions of the law and to serve in the Armed Forces to defend the Union.
Every citizen, with the Union Spirit, has the duty to enhance unity among national races and to ensure public peace and stability.
Every citizen has the duty for the emergence of a modern developed Nation.
Every citizen has the duty to pay taxes to be levied according to the law.
The Union shall enact necessary law to systematically form political parties for flourishing of a genuine, disciplined multi-party democratic system.
Every citizen has the duty to assist the Union in carrying out the following matters: preservation and safeguarding of cultural heritage; environmental conservation; striving for development of human

a. The President or any Vice-President may be impeached for one of the following reasons: high treason; breach of the provisions of this Constitution; misconduct; being disqualified for the President
